Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Documents Filed In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-08-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Seal Documents Filed In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to seal is granted. The declaration establishes that the matter sought to be sealed contains medical records; such records are protected from disclosure under federal and state privacy laws. The court finds that (1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO D/B/A BURLINGAME SKILLED NURSING D/P SNF'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO D/B/A BURLINGAME SKILLED NURSING D/P SNF'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant County of San Mateo d/b/a Burlingame Skilled Nursing d/p SNF's demurrer to plaintiffs Daniel Knight and Estelle Thomas' first amended complaint ("FAC") is sustained without leave to amend.

"The failure to timely present a proper claim for money or damages to a public entity bars a plaintiff from filing a lawsuit against that entity." (Gong v. City of Rosemead (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 363, 374.) Per the court's order denying relief under Gov. Code 946.4, plaintiffs have not complied with the presentation requirements of the Government Claims Act. Their claims against the County are thus barred. The County's request for judicial notice is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

Petition For Leave To Proceed With Action Against County Of San Mateo D/B/A Burlingame Skilled Nursing D/P Snf
Off Calendar Mar-18-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DANIEL KNIGHT, ESTELLE THOMAS' Petition For Leave To Proceed With Action Against County Of San Mateo D/B/A Burlingame Skilled Nursing D/P Snf.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs Daniel Knight and Estelle Thomas' petition for leave to proceed with action is denied.

Lillian Johnson died on July 7, 2022, after an allegedly negligent nursing facility transfer from County of San Mateo d/b/a Burlingame Skilled Nursing Facility d/p SNF ("BSNF"). Plaintiffs, successors in interest, mailed a government claim to BSNF's physical address on November 4, 2022. (Pet. Ex. 1.) Having not heard back, plaintiffs sued for negligence, elder abuse, and wrongful death on May 3, 2023. On June 1, 2023, plaintiffs mailed the County purporting to amend the earlier claim by correcting BSNF's name. (Id. Ex. 2.) The County notified plaintiffs their claim was untimely. (Id. Ex. 3.) Plaintiffs applied for leave to present an amended claim on July 17, 2023, which the County denied. (Id. Ex. 4-5.) Plaintiffs now petition this court for relief.

The Government Claims Act requires those pursuing personal injury claims against a public entity to present their claims within six months of injury. (Gov. Code 911.2.) A claim is "presented" by mail when sent to the "clerk, secretary, auditor, or governing body at its principal office." (Gov. Code 915(a)(2).) Alternatively, a claim is deemed presented if "[i]t is actually received by the clerk, secretary, auditor, or board of the local public entity." (Id. subd. (e)(1).) A claimant may apply for leave to present a late claim "within a reasonable time not to exceed one year" post injury. (Gov. Code 911.4.) The claimant may petition for judicial relief within six months of the application's denial. (Gov. Code 946.4.)

Plaintiffs argue they presented their claim on November 4, 2022, by mailing it to BSNF's physical address. (Pet. Ex. 1.) But as plaintiffs' own record search revealed, this was the wrong service address. At the earliest, plaintiffs presented their claim within the meaning of section 915 on June 1, 2023, when they mailed claim forms to the County, albeit purporting to amend the first mailing. (Id. Ex. 2.) The six-month window had by then expired, and plaintiffs needed to file a section 911.4 application for late claims. Plaintiffs only did so on July 17, 2023, after their one-year window to file a section 911.4 application passed. (Id. Ex. 3.) Having not properly availed themselves of the claims process, plaintiffs are not entitled to relief under Gov. Code 946.6.

Alternatively, plaintiffs argue their claim was deemed presented on November 4, 2022, because the County had actual notice. But plaintiffs lack proof the claim was actually received by a statutorily designated recipient. (Gov. Code 915(e)(1).)

The County's request for consideration of its late-filed opposition is granted. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
